This stage sits at the boundary between session state and the next model call: it assembles the exact prompt-visible input for a turn, both in normal execution and in realtime or debug paths. `turn_context.rs` first freezes mutable session settings into an immutable `TurnContext` snapshot, capturing environment selection, permissions, model metadata, and runtime services for the duration of the turn. From there, `context_manager` turns prior conversation plus context changes into model-ready history: `history.rs` maintains the transcript, token accounting, rollback, and truncation rules; `normalize.rs` repairs malformed call/output sequences and strips unsupported image content; `updates.rs` converts diffs between turn contexts into injected messages describing changed instructions, permissions, collaboration mode, or realtime state; and `mod.rs` exposes that machinery.

Several files contribute incremental context fragments. `additional_context.rs` tracks keyed protocol context and emits only changed entries, while `token_budget.rs` emits discrete “tokens remaining” updates when thresholds are crossed. `contextual_user_message.rs` separates true user text from internally injected contextual content. For realtime flows, `realtime_context.rs` builds a bounded startup summary and `realtime_prompt.rs` selects or renders the backend prompt. `prompt_debug.rs` reproduces this assembly for synthetic inspection, and `ext/web-search/src/history.rs` derives a minimal user-facing history for standalone search.
